title: script_engine.py: circular .scr includes cause infinite recursion — crash
description: master/script_engine.py _run_file() calls itself recursively when a script includes another script via the 'script,name' command. There is no depth counter or cycle detection. A .scr file that directly or indirectly includes itself (A→B→A) will recurse until Python's default stack limit (~1000 frames) is hit, raising RecursionError that crashes the script engine thread. A malicious or accidentally recursive .scr file can take down the Master service. Noted in a comment in the code ('WARNING: recursive') but no protection.
notes: Files: master/script_engine.py _run_file() around line 486. Fix: add a depth parameter defaulting to 0, raise ValueError if depth > MAX_DEPTH (e.g. 10), and pass depth+1 on recursive calls. Or maintain a set of currently-executing script paths and reject re-entry.

title: vesc_can.py: no bounds check before struct.unpack — crash on short VESC packet
description: slave/drivers/vesc_can.py parses VESC telemetry packets using sequential struct.unpack_from() calls with an advancing offset p. There is no check that p + sizeof(field) <= len(payload) before each unpack. If the VESC sends a truncated packet (firmware glitch, CAN noise, partial USB read), unpack_from reads past the buffer end, returning garbage values that get stored as telemetry (temp, current, RPM) — or raises struct.error which propagates uncaught to the telemetry thread and kills it silently. With a dead telemetry thread, vesc_driver stops sending TL:/TR: to the Master, which counts as UART failures and can abort a running choreo.
notes: Files: slave/drivers/vesc_can.py get_values_direct() and get_values_can(). Fix: validate len(payload) >= EXPECTED_MIN_LEN (should be ~54 bytes for full GET_VALUES response) before parsing, and return None immediately on short packet. Add the check once at the top of each function.
close_reason: False positive: get_values_direct() and get_values_can() both already check len(payload) < 54 at lines 188 and 246 before any struct.unpack_from calls. All paths also have try/except Exception.

title: servo_bp: SRV:RELOAD sent to Slave even when SCP failed — Slave reloads stale data
description: Introduced in commit ac52547 (software-fmf fix). In master/api/servo_bp.py _sync_angles_json(), reg.uart.send('SRV','RELOAD') is called unconditionally after the SCP subprocess.run() call, regardless of SCP's return code (check=False). If the Slave Pi is unreachable (WiFi drop, reboot), the new servo_angles.json was never transferred, but the Slave receives RELOAD and reloads its existing (stale) JSON, believing it now has the updated calibration. The user thinks calibration was saved but the Slave is still using old angles.
notes: Files: master/api/servo_bp.py _sync_angles_json() lines 183-190. Fix: check scp result.returncode == 0 before sending RELOAD. Also log a clear warning if SCP fails so the user knows the Slave was not updated.

title: vesc_driver.py: right VESC failure not counted — dead right motor goes undetected
description: slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py telemetry loop: _fail_count is only incremented when get_values_direct() (left/VESC1 USB) returns None. If get_values_can() (right/VESC2 CAN) returns None but get_values_direct() succeeds, TL: is sent but TR: is silently dropped and _fail_count is not incremented. A completely dead right motor/CAN bus causes 0 logged failures and no choreo abort, while the robot drives in circles with one motor. The ChoreoPlayer 3-failure abort threshold never triggers for right-side failures.
notes: Files: slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py _telemetry_loop() around line 385. Fix: only reset _fail_count if BOTH vl and vr are non-None; increment _fail_count if either is None. Or track L/R fail counts independently.

title: settings_bp: _reload_lights_driver sets reg.teeces=None before new driver ready — other threads crash
description: master/api/settings_bp.py _reload_lights_driver() sets reg.teeces = None as a guard during the driver swap. If the new driver's setup() returns False or raises, reg.teeces stays None permanently. Any concurrent request to /teeces/* or any behavior engine tick that calls reg.teeces.random() will crash with AttributeError: 'NoneType' has no attribute 'random'. The old (working) driver is also lost with no fallback path.
notes: Files: master/api/settings_bp.py _reload_lights_driver() lines ~113-128. Fix: keep old driver in a local variable, only replace reg.teeces on successful setup, restore old driver on failure: old = reg.teeces; reg.teeces = new if new.setup() else old.

title: Android: Thread leaks in ping loop and auto-discover — no lifecycle binding
description: MainActivity.kt spawns bare Thread{} in startPingLoop() (~line 137), startAutoDiscover() (~line 173), and discovery dialog (~line 372). These threads are not bound to the Activity lifecycle. On Activity destruction (rotation, back press), threads continue running and call runOnUiThread() on a potentially dead context. Should use lifecycleScope.launch(Dispatchers.IO) or check isDestroyed before runOnUiThread calls. Risk: NullPointerException in edge cases (activity destroyed during network call).

title: motion_bp.py: arcade() endpoint has no UART fallback — silent failure when VESC unavailable
description: master/api/motion_bp.py arcade() endpoint calls reg.vesc.arcade_drive() directly. Unlike the /motion/drive endpoint which falls back to reg.uart.send('M',...) when VESC is not ready, arcade() returns 200 OK with no error and no UART fallback. During bench testing without VESCs connected (common during dev), arcade commands from the Android joystick appear to work (no error) but nothing moves. This is inconsistent with the rest of the motion API and can mask connectivity issues.
notes: Files: master/api/motion_bp.py arcade() endpoint. Fix: add elif reg.uart: reg.uart.send('M', f'{left:.3f},{right:.3f}') fallback after the vesc path, computing differential drive from speed+steering inputs.

title: bt_controller_driver.py: ecodes.BTN.get() can return None — unhandled TypeError crash
description: master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py _handle_button(): the code calls ecodes.BTN.get(event.code) and then checks 'isinstance(raw, (list,tuple))' to handle multi-alias codes. However if event.code is not in BTN (unmapped button on a non-standard gamepad, or a spurious event), get() returns None. Then list(None) or raw[0] raises TypeError which propagates uncaught to the evdev poll loop and kills the BT controller thread — disconnecting the gamepad until the next reconnect cycle (5s). Reproducible with any controller that sends non-standard button codes (e.g. touchpad click on DualShock).
notes: Files: master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py _handle_button() around line 505. Fix: add 'if raw is None: return' immediately after the get() call, before any isinstance check.
close_reason: False positive: _handle_button() already has 'if not raw: return' guard at line 505 immediately after the BTN.get() call, before any list/index operations.

title: slave/main.py: NameError on shutdown if servo or vesc driver init raises exception
description: slave/main.py shutdown sequence (around lines 285-310) calls vesc.shutdown() and servo.shutdown() without guarding against the case where setup raised an exception and the variable was never assigned. If servo.setup() raises (not just returns False) before servo is assigned, the shutdown block hits 'if servo.is_ready(): servo.shutdown()' and raises NameError: name 'servo' is not defined — preventing clean shutdown of all other drivers. Same risk for vesc, display, audio.
notes: Files: slave/main.py driver init and shutdown blocks. Fix: initialize all driver variables to None before their setup() calls (vesc = None, servo = None, etc.) so shutdown guard 'if servo and servo.is_ready()' is safe.

title: safe_stop.py: cancel event cleared before ramp thread starts — rapid commands cause abrupt stop
description: master/safe_stop.py _ramp_to_stop(): _cancel_drive Event is cleared (line ~96) before the ramp thread is started (line ~120). If another thread calls cancel_ramp() in that window — e.g. a new drive command arrives — the event is set before the thread starts. The new thread checks the event immediately, sees it set, and exits without ramping. Motors stop abruptly (0,0 sent directly) instead of the intended 400ms gradual deceleration. Most visible when joystick releases and a new command arrives within ~1ms.
notes: Files: master/safe_stop.py around lines 86-120. Fix: clear the event inside the new thread at its start (before the ramp loop), not before thread creation.
close_reason: False positive: cancel_ramp() in motion_bp.drive() is always immediately followed by the new drive command. The ramp thread exiting due to _cancel_drive means the new drive values take over — no abrupt stop occurs.

title: watchdog.py: elapsed calculated inside lock but checked outside — false VESC cutoff possible
description: slave/watchdog.py _watch_loop(): elapsed and already_triggered are read under self._lock (lines 89-92), but the if-condition that triggers VESC cutoff is evaluated OUTSIDE the lock (line 93). Between the lock release and the if-check, watchdog.feed() can run in another thread: it resets _last_heartbeat and clears _triggered. The main thread then evaluates the stale elapsed (which was >500ms), fires the cutoff, and calls emergency_stop_vesc() even though a fresh heartbeat just arrived. Result: VESCs cut during normal operation if a heartbeat and the watchdog check coincide within a few microseconds.
notes: Files: slave/watchdog.py _watch_loop() lines 86-104. Fix: move the if-condition inside the lock, or re-read _triggered inside the if block to confirm it's still False before firing.

title: JS: choreo poll timer (_pollTimer) keeps running after leaving choreo tab
description: In master/static/js/app.js, _startPolling() creates a setInterval at 500ms to poll /choreo/status. onTabSwitch() (line 459) calls choreoEditor.init() when entering the choreo tab, but does NOT call _stopPolling() when leaving it. The 500ms polling continues on all other tabs until a choreo finishes or the user returns. Fix: call choreoEditor.stop() (or expose _stopPolling) from onTabSwitch() when tabId !== 'choreo'.

title: Android: allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs=true broader than needed
description: MainActivity.kt sets webSettings.allowUniversalAccessFromFileURLs = true and allowFileAccessFromFileURLs = true (lines ~89). For a Pi-only app loading a local IP, this is overly permissive — it allows JS on file:// URLs to read all other file:// URLs without same-origin restriction. Should be false since the app only navigates to http://IP:5000, not file:// origins.

title: local.cfg writes non-atomic — power cycle during write can corrupt config
description: All blueprints that persist settings to local.cfg use a read-modify-write pattern with plain open('w') (settings_bp._write_key, vesc_bp._save_vesc_cfg, servo_bp arms config, behavior_bp). This truncates the file then writes the new content. If the process crashes, the Pi loses power, or the SD card has a write error between truncate and close(), local.cfg is left partially written or empty. On next boot, config_loader.py silently falls back to main.cfg defaults for all keys — robot boots with factory settings (power_scale=1.0, no arms config, no behavior settings, etc.) with no error shown to the user.
notes: Files: master/api/settings_bp.py:66-73 (_write_key), master/api/vesc_bp.py:49-59 (_save_vesc_cfg), master/api/servo_bp.py:398-411 (arms), master/api/behavior_bp.py:91-129. Fix direction: atomic write pattern — write to local.cfg.tmp then os.replace('local.cfg.tmp', 'local.cfg'). os.replace() is atomic on POSIX (rename syscall). Optionally add f.flush() + os.fsync(f.fileno()) before close for SD card safety. A single shared _write_cfg_atomic(path, cfg) helper in config_loader.py would fix all call sites at once.
close_reason: Fixed in commits 6169061+94b54b8 (atomic writes), 3b82dc3 (audio queue worker), ac52547 (servo reload)

title: Servo angles cached at setup() — calibration changes require service restart
description: dome_servo_driver.py loads dome_angles.json once at setup() into self._angles (line 128). After that, the cache is never invalidated. When the user saves new calibration via POST /servo/settings, servo_bp writes the JSON to disk and SCPs to Slave — but the running driver still holds the old angles in memory. The new values only take effect after a full service restart. Same issue applies to body_servo_driver on the Slave.
notes: Files: master/drivers/dome_servo_driver.py:99-128 (_load_dome_angles, called once at setup). master/api/servo_bp.py:141-186 (writes JSON but does not notify driver). Fix direction: add a reload() method to DomeServoDriver and BodyServoDriver that re-reads the JSON file and updates self._angles. servo_bp should call reg.dome_servo.reload() and notify Slave to reload body servos (new UART command SRV:RELOAD or direct driver call) after writing the JSON files. Alternatively, make _get_angle() read from disk each time (simpler but adds I/O on every servo command).
close_reason: Fixed in commits 6169061+94b54b8 (atomic writes), 3b82dc3 (audio queue worker), ac52547 (servo reload)

title: Audio Popen callbacks block UART thread — potential watchdog race
description: In slave/uart_listener.py, all UART callbacks are dispatched synchronously inline in the uart-slave-rx thread (_process_line:192). The audio driver's _launch() calls subprocess.Popen() inside a threading.Lock while holding the UART thread. If 6 audio channels are launched in rapid succession (e.g. dense choreo start), the H: heartbeat frame stays buffered in the OS serial buffer and is not processed until all Popen() calls return. Since watchdog.feed() is registered as a callback for H: (slave/main.py:168), both the ACK and the watchdog feed are delayed by the stacked Popen latency. Timing analysis: Popen on Pi 4B = 5-80ms per call. 6 calls x 80ms = 480ms blocking. Watchdog timeout = 500ms. Heartbeat interval = 200ms. Worst case total delay = 680ms > 500ms — watchdog fires and cuts VESCs mid-choreo. Risk is low under normal load (Popen ~10ms x6 = 60ms, well within margin) but becomes a real race under heavy system load (6 mpg123 processes already running + VESC telemetry + choreo computation).
notes: Files involved: slave/uart_listener.py:120-196 (_read_loop, _process_line) — slave/drivers/audio_driver.py:208-226 (_launch, subprocess.Popen inside lock) — slave/watchdog.py:86-104 (_watch_loop) — slave/main.py:168 (watchdog.feed registered as H callback). Fix direction: add a queue.Queue + dedicated audio worker thread in AudioDriver. UART callback posts (channel, path, volume) to the queue and returns immediately. Worker thread consumes queue and calls Popen. This decouples Popen latency from the UART thread entirely. Alternative (simpler but less clean): call watchdog.feed() hardcoded inline in _process_line before the callbacks loop, same as the H:OK ACK — this eliminates the feed delay regardless of callback duration.
close_reason: Fixed in commits 6169061+94b54b8 (atomic writes), 3b82dc3 (audio queue worker), ac52547 (servo reload)

value: AUDIO CHANNELS CONFIG (vérifié settings_bp.py 2026-04-28): Endpoint: POST /settings/config avec {audio.channels: N}. Séquence complète: (1) write local.cfg [audio] audio_channels (2) lit slave.cfg local (3) write slave.cfg [audio] audio_channels (4) SCP slave.cfg → artoo@r2-slave.local:/home/artoo/r2d2/slave/config/slave.cfg (5) thread daemon sleep 2s → systemctl restart r2d2-slave ET r2d2-master. Slave lit au boot: slave/main.py:176 _read_audio_channels() → slave.cfg getint('audio','audio_channels',fallback=6). IMPACT: tous les sons en cours sont coupés au restart. Default=6, range 1-12 (local.cfg), hardcoded fallback=6. JAMAIS changer audio_channels sans accepter un restart complet des deux services.

AUDIO SLAVE (vérifié slave/drivers/audio_driver.py): déclenchement via UART commandes S: depuis le Master. Format: S:filename:CRC (channel 0), S:RANDOM:category:CRC, S:STOP:CRC, S2:/S3: etc pour autres channels. Backend: mpg123 via subprocess. Sons MP3 dans /home/artoo/r2d2/slave/sounds/ (gitignorés, 317 fichiers). Volume ALSA: amixer -c 0 cset numid=1 <vol>%. Channels audio: 6 par défaut (slave/config/slave.cfg).

UART CRC CORRECTION (vérifié shared/uart_protocol.py:66): formule réelle = (sum(payload_bytes) + len(payload_bytes)) % 256, PAS juste sum % 256. CLAUDE.md ligne 'def calc_crc' est inexact — le +len() est intentionnel pour détecter insertion de null bytes (slipring BREAK → 0x00). Format message: TYPE:VALUE:CS\n, CS = 2 hex majuscules. Exemple: build_msg('H','1') → 'H:1:XX\n'.

VESC HARDWARE (vérifié slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py:88-89): VESC_ID_USB=1 (Flipsky Mini V6.7 connecté Pi Slave via USB, ttyACM auto-detect dans [ttyACM0,ttyACM1,ttyACM2], skip le port du RP2040). VESC_ID_CAN=2 (atteint via COMM_FORWARD_CAN à travers VESC1). MAX_ERPM=50000 par défaut. HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT=0.85. Télémétrie TL:/TR: toutes les 200ms. ATTENTION: 'Multiple ESC over CAN' DOIT être DÉSACTIVÉ sur les deux VESCs.

ANDROID APP (vérifié android/app/src/main/ 2026-04-28): WebView wrapper Kotlin — PAS d'app native. Charge fichiers locaux bundlés: index.html (tablette >=600dp) ou mobile.html (mobile <600dp) depuis file:///android_asset/. Communication: REST HTTP uniquement, PAS de WebSockets. Heartbeat POST /heartbeat toutes les 200ms (mobile.js:30). Status poll GET /status toutes les 1000ms. Discovery: mDNS r2-master.local → IP sauvegardée → 192.168.4.1 → scan subnet 254 IPs en 50 threads. IP configurée dans SharedPreferences 'r2d2_prefs' clé 'host' (défaut 192.168.4.1). Port fixe 5000. Assets copiés depuis master/static/ — doivent être synchronisés manuellement si JS/CSS change.

ARCHITECTURE MATÉRIELLE VÉRIFIÉE (source code 2026-04-28): Master=Pi 4B (dome, 192.168.2.104), Slave=Pi 4B 2GB (body, 192.168.4.171). UART physique: /dev/ttyAMA0 @ 115200 baud sur les deux Pis (shared/uart_protocol.py BAUD_RATE=115200). Heartbeat Master→Slave toutes les 200ms (HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS). Watchdog Slave: timeout 500ms (WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS) → emergency_stop_vesc() + affiche SYSTEM_LOCKED sur RP2040.

PROPAGATION CONFIG → DRIVERS (vérifié master/api/): Pattern A — write local.cfg direct (settings_bp, vesc_bp, behavior_bp). Pattern B — write JSON + SCP vers Slave (servo_bp: dome_angles.json local, servo_angles.json via SCP). Pattern C — registry only, pas persisté (ex: vesc duty mode). Pattern D — hot-reload avec swap atomique (lights backend: shutdown old driver, load new, swap reg.teeces). Pattern E — write + SCP + systemctl restart (audio channels: redémarre r2d2-slave via subprocess 2s délai). Power scale et invert VESC: UART immédiat (VCFG/VINV) + registry, PAS de restart. Servo angles: lazy load (driver re-lit JSON au prochain appel).

VESC PROPULSION CONFIG (vérifié 2026-04-28): MAX_ERPM=50000 défini slave/drivers/vesc_driver.py:94 — PAS modifiable UI, seulement UART VCFG:erpm:<N> (min 1000, pas persisté). HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT=0.85 défini vesc_driver.py:97 — hardcodé, pas configurable. Chaîne calcul ERPM: input[-1..1] × power_scale → clamp HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT → × max_erpm → VESC. power_scale: défaut 1.0 (registry.py:64), lu au boot depuis local.cfg [vesc] power_scale (main.py:217), UART VCFG:scale + local.cfg à chaque changement UI. Freinage/rampe: master/safe_stop.py — RAMP_MAX_MS=400, RAMP_STEP_MS=20, DEADZONE=0.08 — software-side uniquement, pas firmware VESC. Seuils choreo: vesc_max_current=30A, vesc_max_temp=80°C, vesc_min_voltage=cells×3.5V (choreo_player.py:135-145) — local.cfg [choreo] seulement, PAS d'UI. Modifiable UI: power_scale, invert_L/R (persistés), duty_mode (volatile). Non modifiable UI: MAX_ERPM, HARDWARE_SPEED_LIMIT, seuils de courant/temp.

WEB CONFIG ENGINE (vérifié 2026-04-28): Fichiers de config — main.cfg (defaults, dans repo), local.cfg (overrides, gitignorée), dome_angles.json (gitignorée), servo_angles.json (Slave, gitignorée), choreo_categories.json (dans repo). Chargement au boot: config_loader.py merge main.cfg + local.cfg. Sections local.cfg: [behavior][github][home_wifi][hotspot][slave][admin][lights][audio][battery][vesc][arms]. Blueprints: audio/motion/servo/script/status/teeces/settings/vesc/bt/light/choreo/camera/behavior dans master/api/. Registry (master/registry.py) = conteneur d'injection de dépendances — drivers + état partagés (reg.uart, reg.vesc, reg.vesc_power_scale, reg.web_last_drive_t, reg.estop_active, reg.lock_mode, etc.).

I2C BUS (vérifié source): Master PCA9685 @ 0x40 = 16 servos dôme (master/drivers/dome_servo_driver.py:57). Slave PCA9685 @ 0x41 = 16 servos body (slave/drivers/body_servo_driver.py:56). Les deux sur I2C bus 1. Pulse: 500µs + (angle/180)*2000µs.

NEVER hardcode installation-specific values (IPs, hostnames, paths, credentials) in Python code. Always read from local.cfg. The pattern: configparser.ConfigParser().read([MAIN_CFG, LOCAL_CFG]).get('section', 'key', fallback='default'). Offenders caught: servo_bp hardcoded 192.168.4.171 instead of reading local.cfg [slave] host. Reference files that already do it right: settings_bp.py (_read_cfg), vesc_bp.py (_LOCAL_CFG), behavior_bp.py (_get_cfg). Default fallback should be the .local mDNS name (r2-slave.local) for portability, NOT a fixed IP.

PERSISTANCE SETTINGS (vérifié config_loader.py + blueprints 2026-04-28): Priorité: local.cfg OVERRIDE main.cfg (ConfigParser.read() séquentiel, dernier gagne). Séquence boot master: load() cfg → logging → network monitor → git pull → Phase1 drivers (UART/lights/deploy) → load VESC registry depuis cfg (main.py:217-219) → Phase2 servos → Phase3 ScriptEngine/ChoreoPlayer → BehaviorEngine → Flask. Pattern write: read-modify-write fichier entier (PAS atomique — pas de rename/fsync, truncate+write direct). Pas de cache write-through — chaque blueprint relit le fichier. Survie reboot: VESC scale/invert → local.cfg [vesc] ✓ | Servo angles → JSON files ✓ | Audio channels → slave.cfg via SCP ✓ | Behavior → local.cfg [behavior] ✓ | VESC duty_mode → registry volatile, PERDU au reboot ✗. Risque: écriture non-atomique de local.cfg — crash mid-write = fichier corrompu possible (rare mais non protégé).

BLUETOOTH GAMEPAD (vérifié master/drivers/bt_controller_driver.py 2026-04-28): Evdev-based, ~650 lignes. Boutons: BTN_EAST(305)=audio RANDOM:happy, BTN_WEST(304)=dome servo open/close, BTN_NORTH(307)=body servo open/close, BTN_MODE(316)=E-Stop (toujours actif). Axes: ABS_Y(1)=throttle (Y inversé: up=avant), ABS_X(0)=steer, ABS_RX(3)=dome. Deadzone=0.10. Differential drive: left=throttle+steer, right=throttle-steer*0.55 (amortissement 55%). Kids mode: multiplie par kids_speed_limit. Child Lock (lock_mode=2): bloque toute propulsion. Keep-alive thread: renvoie dernier (left,right) toutes les 300ms si _drive_active et |val|>0.01 (évite motion watchdog 800ms). Auto-reconnect: scan evdev toutes les 5s. Pairing via REST POST /bt/pair → bluetoothctl subprocess. bt_config.json n'existe pas encore — utilise defaults hardcodés.

GESTION PRIORITÉS MULTI-CANAL (vérifié registry.py + bt_controller_driver.py 2026-04-28): Trois canaux concurrents: Web/Android (REST Flask), Gamepad BT (evdev direct), ChoreoPlayer (timer thread). Mécanisme priorité Web > BT: reg.web_last_drive_t et reg.web_last_dome_t (timestamps monotoniques) mis à jour par motion_bp.py à chaque commande web. BT controller vérifie: si time()-web_last_drive_t < 0.5s → skip la commande gamepad. Fenêtre priorité web = 500ms. Pas de mutex ni queue — accès concurrent aux drivers directs. BT et Choreo peuvent se chevaucher (pas de lock entre eux). E-Stop (BTN_MODE) contourne toutes les priorités — traité avant tout check.

RP2040 DISPLAY (vérifié slave/drivers/display_driver.py): port auto-détecté parmi ttyACM0/ttyACM1/ttyACM2 — le premier disponible est pris par RP2040, les suivants par les VESCs. Firmware MicroPython GC9A01 dans rp2040/firmware/. Flasher: mpremote rm :display.py PUIS cp (jamais cp seul). Affiche SYSTEM_LOCKED quand watchdog UART fire.

SERVOS/DÔME CALIBRATION (vérifié 2026-04-28): Fichiers: master/config/dome_angles.json (Servo_M0..M15), slave/config/servo_angles.json (Servo_S0..S15). Structure par servo: {label(max40chars), open(int 10-170°), close(int 10-170°), speed(int 1-10)}. Pas d'offset — angles absolus uniquement. Defaults hardcodés: open=110, close=20, speed=10 (dome_servo_driver.py:63-66). Chargement: une seule fois au setup() → cache self._angles, PAS relu à chaque commande. Fallback silencieux si clé manquante. Validation à l'écriture: _clamp() [10,170], _clamp_speed() [1,10], pas d'exception — silently clamped. SCP vers Slave: subprocess scp timeout=5s, artoo@r2-slave.local:/home/artoo/r2d2/slave/config/servo_angles.json (servo_bp.py:170-186). Déclencheur: POST /servo/settings.
